repo_notes.content: Module tags are the lowercased Task enum variant in src/registry/list.rs. `!yum` and `!dnf` are two tags for the same YumDnfTask implementation. These pages are regenerated by `jetpack gen-reference` — edit this file, not the pages under docs/content/docs/reference/.
repo_notes.author: jetpack

repo_notes.content: Parameter types are derived from how each module uses the field (via the templating helpers in evaluate), not the Rust field type — most fields are Option<String> in the struct but are really bool/int/path/list/map at runtime. Universal fields name/with/and are documented under Task Modifiers, not repeated per module.
repo_notes.author: jetpack

group.description: Manage a Linux group via groupadd/groupmod/groupdel/gpasswd. Linux-only.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group name to manage.

parameters.name: gid
parameters.description: Desired numeric GID.

parameters.name: users
parameters.description: Member usernames, managed via gpasswd -M.

parameters.name: append
parameters.description: When modifying users, add to existing members rather than replacing.

parameters.name: system
parameters.description: Create a system group (groupadd -r), only when no gid given.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the group instead of creating it.

examples:
- !group
  group: developers
  gid: 5000
  users:
    - alice
    - bob
  append: true

user.description: Manage a Linux user account via useradd/usermod/userdel. Linux-only.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Username to manage.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: Desired numeric UID.

parameters.name: system
parameters.description: Create a system user (useradd -r), only when no uid given.

parameters.name: gid
parameters.description: Primary group name or GID.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Supplementary group names; compared against id -Gn.

parameters.name: append
parameters.description: When modifying groups, add to existing rather than replacing.

parameters.name: create_home
parameters.description: Create the home directory (-m vs -M). Default true.

parameters.name: create_user_group
parameters.description: Create a group with the same name as the user (-U vs -N). Default true.

parameters.name: gecos
parameters.description: GECOS/comment field (-c).

parameters.name: shell
parameters.description: Login shell (-s).

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the user instead of creating.

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.description: When removing, also delete the home directory (userdel -r).

examples:
- !user
  user: deploy
  uid: 2000
  groups:
    - www-data
    - docker
  append: true
  shell: /bin/bash

command.description: Run a single command on the target directly, without a shell. Safer than !shell (no injection) but no pipes, redirection, or builtins unless unsafe: true.
notes: Forbidden unless unsafe: ; < > & * ? { } [ ] $ backtick — and no pipes or shell builtins.

parameters.name: cmd
parameters.description: The command line to execute (templated).

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Variable name to save { rc, out } from the run.

parameters.name: failed_when
parameters.description: Expression overriding the default fail-when-rc-nonzero.

parameters.name: changed_when
parameters.description: Expression overriding the default always-changed.

parameters.name: unsafe
parameters.description: Allow shell metacharacters in a templated cmd. Default false; auto-true when cmd has no templating.

examples:
- !command
  name: check disk usage
  cmd: df -h /
  save: disk_result

external.description: Run an external (user-supplied) program module on the target. The module binary is uploaded and invoked with the JSON params piped to stdin; it must emit a YAML/JSON map on stdout.

parameters.name: use
parameters.description: Name/path of the external module program to locate and upload.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Free-form parameter map, serialized to JSON and fed to the module stdin.

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Variable name to save the parsed response map (always includes rc).

parameters.name: failed_when
parameters.description: Expression overriding fail-when-rc-nonzero.

parameters.name: changed_when
parameters.description: Expression overriding always-changed.

examples:
- !external
  use: my_health_check
  params:
    endpoint: /api/v1/status
    timeout: 5
  save: health

shell.description: Run a command through a real shell (default /bin/bash), enabling pipes, redirects, and builtins.

parameters.name: cmd
parameters.description: The shell command line to execute (templated; passed to <shell> -c).

parameters.name: shell
parameters.description: Path to the shell binary (e.g. /bin/zsh). Default /bin/bash.

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Variable name to save { rc, out, stdout }.

parameters.name: failed_when
parameters.description: Expression overriding fail-when-rc-nonzero.

parameters.name: changed_when
parameters.description: Expression overriding always-changed.

examples:
- !shell
  cmd: ps aux | wc -l
  save: proc_count

assert.description: Abort the play (fail the task) when one or more templated condition expressions evaluate incorrectly.

parameters.name: msg
parameters.description: Custom message shown when the assertion fails.

parameters.name: true
parameters.description: A condition expression that must be true.

parameters.name: false
parameters.description: A condition expression that must be false.

parameters.name: all_true
parameters.description: Every expression in the list must be true.

parameters.name: all_false
parameters.description: Every expression in the list must be false.

parameters.name: some_true
parameters.description: At least one expression in the list must be true.

examples:
- !assert
  name: Ensure enough disk space
  true: (gt disk_free_pct 20)
  msg: free disk below 20%

debug.description: Print the host's current variables (the templating context) for debugging; does not change host state.

parameters.name: vars
parameters.description: Restrict output to only these variable names; omit to dump all.

echo.description: Log a templated message to the run output; no host changes.

parameters.name: msg
parameters.description: Message to print (template-expanded).

facts.description: Gather OS, architecture, and (optionally) facter/ohai facts on the target and store them as host variables (jet_os_type, jet_os_flavor, jet_arch, jet_os_release_*). Must run before any jet_* variable is used. See the facts module page for the full variable reference.

parameters.name: facter
parameters.description: Also run facter --json and store results under the facter variable (requires facter).

parameters.name: ohai
parameters.description: Also run ohai and store results under the ohai variable (requires ohai).

examples:
- !facts
  name: gather system facts
  facter: yes

fail.description: Unconditionally fail the task (and stop the play) with an optional message.

parameters.name: msg
parameters.description: Failure message; defaults to fail invoked.

self_locate.description: Introspect the target's virtualization environment (lxc/qemu/pod/physical) and workload ID, saving the result as a host variable map.

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Variable name under which the detection result map is stored.

parameters.name: api_host
parameters.description: Proxmox API host URL (reserved for future use).

parameters.name: api_token_id
parameters.description: Proxmox API token ID (reserved for future use).

parameters.name: api_token_secret
parameters.description: Proxmox API token secret (reserved for future use).

set.description: Set one or more host variables. Top-level string values are template-expanded; non-string values are copied verbatim.

parameters.name: vars
parameters.description: Mapping of variable name to value.

wait_for_host.description: Poll the target over SSH until it responds, useful after provisioning VMs/LXCs. Fails on timeout.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum seconds to wait for connectivity. Default 300.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Seconds between retry attempts. Default 5.

examples:
- !wait_for_host
  name: Wait for new VM to accept SSH
  timeout: 600
  delay: 10

wait_for_http.description: Poll an HTTP endpoint until it responds (optionally with a specific status code), useful for waiting on an API or service to become ready after boot. Fails on timeout.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The HTTP(S) URL to poll.

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected HTTP status code (e.g. 200). If omitted, any 2xx is accepted.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum seconds to wait. Default 300.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Seconds between polls. Default 5.

parameters.name: verify_tls
parameters.description: Verify the TLS certificate. Default false.

wait_for_others.description: Barrier synchronization point for --async mode; all hosts must reach this task before any proceed past it. Silently skipped in non-async mode.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Barrier mode: strict or loose. Default loose.

examples:
- !wait_for_others
  name: all nodes have rqlite installed
  mode: strict

copy.description: Copy a file (or recursively a directory tree) from the control machine to the target. Idempotent via SHA-512 content comparison.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Local source path (a directory when recursive: true; otherwise resolved through files/ lookup).

parameters.name: dest
parameters.description: Remote destination path.

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Recursively copy an entire local directory tree to dest.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: File attributes applied to copied files: owner, group, mode.

directory.description: Manage a directory on the target — create, remove, or adjust attributes. Fails if the path is an existing regular file.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path of the directory to manage.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the directory if it exists.

parameters.name: recurse
parameters.description: Apply attribute changes recursively into the directory contents.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: File attributes: owner, group, mode.

examples:
- !directory
  path: /srv/app/data
  recurse: true
  attributes:
    owner: app
    mode: '0o755'

download.description: Download a URL to a remote path on the target using curl (must be installed). Not idempotent unless the file is absent — set force to re-fetch.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to download.

parameters.name: dest
parameters.description: Remote destination path (parent dir created if missing).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Octal permission string applied via chmod after download.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: Owner applied via chown after download.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group applied via chown after download.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Re-download even if dest already exists.

fetch.description: Retrieve a file from the target to the control machine (the inverse of copy). dest is optional; content is also exposed via the run result.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Remote path to fetch (must exist).

parameters.name: dest
parameters.description: Local destination path. If omitted, content is captured via the run result.

file.description: Manage the state of a regular file on the target — create/touch, remove, or adjust attributes (owner/group/mode). Fails if the path is a directory.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path of the file to manage.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the file if it exists.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: File attributes: owner, group, mode (octal, requires 0o or 0 prefix).

git.description: Clone or update a git repository on the target. Idempotent — compares local HEAD/branch against remote and updates only when needed. SSH key auth only.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: Repository URL (https/http or ssh style).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Remote destination directory for the checkout.

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: Branch to checkout/switch to. Default main.

parameters.name: ssh_options
parameters.description: Map of extra SSH options passed as -o KEY=VALUE.

parameters.name: accept_keys
parameters.description: Auto-accept new SSH host keys via StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new. Default true.

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Pull from remote if local HEAD differs. Default true.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: File attributes applied to the checkout directory: owner, group, mode.

examples:
- !git
  repo: git@github.com:example/app.git
  path: /srv/app
  branch: release
  ssh_options:
    IdentityFile: ~/.ssh/deploy_key

move.description: Move/rename a file on the target. Optionally backs up an existing destination first. Supports force-overwrite and post-move permission/ownership changes.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Remote source path (must exist).

parameters.name: dest
parameters.description: Remote destination path (parent dir must exist).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Octal permission string applied via chmod after the move.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: Owner applied via chown after the move.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group applied via chown after the move.

parameters.name: backup
parameters.description: Copy the existing dest to a timestamped .backup before overwriting.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Overwrite an existing destination instead of reporting matched.

stat.description: Stat a remote path and save the result (exists, is_dir, mode, owner, group) into a host variable for later use. Read-only; never modifies the target.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Remote path to inspect.

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Variable name under which the stat result map is stored.

template.description: Render a local template (resolved through the templates/ lookup) with variables and write the result to a remote path. Idempotent via SHA-512 of rendered output.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Local template filename (looked up under the templates directory).

parameters.name: dest
parameters.description: Remote destination path for the rendered file.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: File attributes applied to the rendered file: owner, group, mode.

unpack.description: Extract an archive (tar.gz/tgz/tar.bz2/tar.xz/tar/zip, or single-file gz/bz2/xz) on the target into a destination directory. Source archive must exist on the target.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Remote path to the archive (format inferred from extension).

parameters.name: dest
parameters.description: Remote destination directory (created if missing).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Octal permission string applied recursively via chmod -R after extraction.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: Owner applied recursively via chown -R after extraction.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group applied recursively via chown -R after extraction.

examples:
- !unpack
  src: /opt/app/app.tar.gz
  dest: /opt/app
  owner: app
  group: app

github_release.description: Query the GitHub release API and store the best-matching release version (semver-parsed) into a host variable. Passive — resolves and records the version string, does not download assets.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: GitHub repo in owner/name form (e.g. ipfs/kubo).

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Release channel: stable/latest (non-prerelease), unstable (prereleases), prerelease (smart: prefers stable of same version over its RC), any. Default latest.

parameters.name: version_filter
parameters.description: Cargo-style semver requirement to constrain candidates (e.g. ^18.0.0, >=2.0.0).

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Host-fact var name to write the resolved version into (use save XOR set).

parameters.name: set
parameters.description: Magic var name to write the resolved version into (use set XOR save).

examples:
- !github_release
  repo: ipfs/kubo
  channel: stable
  version_filter: ^0.20.0
  save: ipfs_version

instantiate.description: Generate inventory host_vars files and group memberships for a fleet of machines (runs on LOCAL inventory). Triggers the provision system by writing a provision block per host. Supports hostname-pattern expansion and round-robin node distribution.

parameters.name: inventory_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the inventory directory.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Hostname pattern; expands {01..10} (zero-padded range) or {a,b,c} (list).

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.description: Proxmox node names to distribute hosts across (round-robin).

parameters.name: provision
parameters.description: Provision template; required keys type (proxmox_vm/proxmox_lxc) and cluster.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Group names to add the generated hosts to.

parameters.name: vmid_start
parameters.description: Starting VMID; assigned sequentially per host. Omit for Proxmox auto-assign.

parameters.name: ip_template
parameters.description: IP template with {} placeholder (e.g. 10.7.1.{}/24) or literal dhcp.

parameters.name: ip_start
parameters.description: Number substituted into ip_template for the first host; increments. Default 1.

parameters.name: gateway
parameters.description: Gateway IP; baked into net0 for LXC.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: present (create/update, default) or absent (remove host_vars and prune groups).

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Var name to record the generated hostname list into.

examples:
- !instantiate
  inventory_path: /opt/jetpack/inventory
  pattern: fleet-{01..05}.lon.riff.cc
  nodes: [bee, moth]
  vmid_start: 200
  ip_template: 10.7.1.{}/24
  ip_start: 230
  gateway: 10.7.1.1
  groups: [testfleet]
  provision:
    type: proxmox_lxc
    cluster: SpaceTempAgency
    memory: 2048
    cores: 4

apt.description: Manage packages with apt-get/dpkg (Debian/Ubuntu). Supports specific versions or local .deb paths, and auto-refreshes the apt cache.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Package name, or a local .deb file path.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Pin a specific version (apt-get install name=version).

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Force an apt-get update before acting.

parameters.name: upgrade
parameters.description: Treat as an upgrade operation (--only-upgrade).

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the package instead of installing.

dnf.description: Manage packages with yum or dnf (RHEL-family). Identical to !yum — both map to YumDnfTask; the backend is auto-detected per host (prefers /usr/bin/dnf, falls back to /usr/bin/yum).

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Package name.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Pin a specific version (appended as name-version).

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Run update semantics (compares against repoquery candidate).

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the package.

homebrew.description: Manage packages with Homebrew (brew) on macOS/Linuxbrew. Locates the brew binary automatically (including /opt/homebrew/bin/brew).

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Package (formula) name.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Install a specific major version via name@version.

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Use brew upgrade semantics.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Uninstall the package (brew uninstall).

pacman.description: Manage packages with pacman (Arch Linux). Accepts repo/name syntax (strips the repo prefix for queries/removal).

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Package name (optionally repo/name).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Pin a version (appended as -version).

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Run pacman -Syu upgrade semantics.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the package (pacman -R).

yum.description: Manage packages with yum or dnf (RHEL-family). Same module as !dnf — the backend is auto-detected per host (prefers /usr/bin/dnf, falls back to /usr/bin/yum).

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Package name.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Pin a specific version (appended as name-version).

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Run update semantics (compares against repoquery candidate).

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the package.

zypper.description: Manage packages with zypper (openSUSE/SUSE). Uses zypper search --details for version comparison.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Package name.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Pin a specific version (name=version).

parameters.name: update
parameters.description: Use zypper update semantics.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.description: Remove the package (zypper remove).

proxmox_lxc.description: Manage a Proxmox LXC container via the API (create/remove). Self-signed certs accepted. Idempotent on state.

parameters.name: api_host
parameters.description: Proxmox API host (hostname or IP; port 8006 implied).

parameters.name: api_token_id
parameters.description: Proxmox API token ID.

parameters.name: api_token_secret
parameters.description: Proxmox API token secret.

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: Proxmox node name to place the container on.

parameters.name: vmid
parameters.description: Container VMID.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Container hostname.

parameters.name: ostemplate
parameters.description: Template storage path (e.g. local:vztmpl/debian-12.tar.zst).

parameters.name: storage
parameters.description: Root-disk storage pool. Default local-lvm.

parameters.name: memory
parameters.description: Memory in MB. Default 512.

parameters.name: cores
parameters.description: CPU core count. Default 1.

parameters.name: rootfs_size
parameters.description: Rootfs size string appended to storage. Default 8G.

parameters.name: net0
parameters.description: Raw Proxmox net0 config string.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Root password.

parameters.name: unprivileged
parameters.description: Unprivileged container. Default true.

parameters.name: start_on_create
parameters.description: Start container immediately after creation.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: present (create if missing, default) or absent (stop + delete).

examples:
- !proxmox_lxc
  api_host: '{{ proxmox_host }}'
  api_token_id: '{{ proxmox_token_id }}'
  api_token_secret: '{{ proxmox_token_secret }}'
  node: bee
  vmid: 201
  hostname: app01
  ostemplate: local:vztmpl/debian-12-standard.tar.zst
  memory: 2048
  cores: 2
  start_on_create: true
  state: present

proxmox_migrate.description: Live (online) or offline migration of a Proxmox VM or LXC guest from a source node to a target node. Blocks until the migration task completes or times out.

parameters.name: api_host
parameters.description: Proxmox API host.

parameters.name: api_token_id
parameters.description: Proxmox API token ID.

parameters.name: api_token_secret
parameters.description: Proxmox API token secret.

parameters.name: source_node
parameters.description: Node the guest currently resides on.

parameters.name: target_node
parameters.description: Node to migrate the guest to.

parameters.name: vmid
parameters.description: Guest VMID.

parameters.name: guest_type
parameters.description: vm/qemu or lxc/container.

parameters.name: online
parameters.description: For VMs: live migration. Default true.

parameters.name: restart
parameters.description: For LXCs: restart guest after migration. Default true.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Seconds to wait for migration to finish. Default 300.

examples:
- !proxmox_migrate
  api_host: '{{ proxmox_host }}'
  api_token_id: '{{ proxmox_token_id }}'
  api_token_secret: '{{ proxmox_token_secret }}'
  source_node: bee
  target_node: moth
  vmid: 100
  guest_type: lxc
  online: true

proxmox_node.description: Passive/read-only query of Proxmox node status (uptime, load, memory, CPU), optionally with cluster quorum and VM/LXC lists. Saves a JSON blob into a host variable.

parameters.name: api_host
parameters.description: Proxmox API host.

parameters.name: api_token_id
parameters.description: Proxmox API token ID.

parameters.name: api_token_secret
parameters.description: Proxmox API token secret.

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: Node name to query.

parameters.name: save_to
parameters.description: Variable name to store the resulting JSON info string into.

parameters.name: include_cluster
parameters.description: Also fetch /cluster/status (adds cluster list + quorate bool).

parameters.name: include_vms
parameters.description: Also list QEMU VMs on the node (adds vms + vm_count).

parameters.name: include_lxc
parameters.description: Also list LXCs on the node (adds lxc + lxc_count).

examples:
- !proxmox_node
  api_host: '{{ proxmox_host }}'
  api_token_id: '{{ proxmox_token_id }}'
  api_token_secret: '{{ proxmox_token_secret }}'
  node: bee
  save_to: node_info
  include_cluster: true
  include_vms: true

sd_service.description: Manage a systemd service's enabled/started state via systemctl. Conflicts (started:false + restart:true) fail; restart without started implies start-if-stopped.

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: systemd unit name (e.g. nginx.service).

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Desired enablement state (systemctl enable/disable). If omitted, left unchanged.

parameters.name: started
parameters.description: Desired running state (systemctl start/stop). If omitted with restart, restarts if running or starts if stopped.

parameters.name: restart
parameters.description: Restart the service if it is (or should be) running (systemctl restart).

examples:
- !sd_service
  service: nginx
  enabled: true
  started: true

check-local.description: Show configuration differences on the local machine (no changes).

ssh.description: Manage multiple remote hosts over SSH.

check-ssh.description: Show configuration differences over SSH (no changes).

pull.description: Pull and apply configuration locally, with optional inventory for variables.

show-inventory.description: Display resolved inventory hosts and groups.

inventory-check.description: Validate an inventory tree (groups, group_vars, host_vars).

full-check.description: Run syntax-check and inventory-check in one pass.

docs.description: Build and serve this documentation site locally.

install.description: Install the running jetpack binary to /usr/local/bin/jetpack and symlink /usr/local/bin/jetp to it.

-p.description: Playbook path(s); the automation content to run.

--playbook.description: Playbook path(s); the automation content to run.

-i.description: Inventory path; may be given multiple times (later paths overlay earlier ones).

--inventory.description: Inventory path; may be given multiple times (later paths overlay earlier ones).

--limit-hosts.description: Restrict the run to these hosts.

--limit-groups.description: Restrict the run to these groups.

--port.description: SSH port (ssh modes) or HTTP port (docs mode).

--sudo.description: Sudo to this user for all tasks by default.

--tags.description: Only run tasks/roles with one of these tags.

-e.description: Inject extra variables from a YAML file or quoted JSON.

--extra-vars.description: Inject extra variables from a YAML file or quoted JSON.

--no-browser.description: (docs mode) Print the local docs URL instead of opening a browser.

--forward-agent.description: Enable SSH agent forwarding for specific tasks (e.g. git).
